title: Decoder attention softmax weight generator PPA sweep
hypothesis: Measured softmax weight generation cost is small enough relative to the nm64 compute-array budget that the current Llama7B full-value attention frontier remains stable, but the q12 PWL variant may expose a non-negligible area or power penalty versus the q8 reciprocal-normalized path.

direct_comparison.primary_question: What area, power, and timing should the L2 Llama7B attention schedule charge for score-to-weight softmax generation?
include: q8 shift-exp reciprocal-normalized rowwise softmax with reciprocal widths 10, 12, 14, and 16
q12 PWL-exp reciprocal-normalized rowwise softmax with reciprocal widths 10, 12, 14, and 16
Nangate45 macro-style high-utilization sweep points at core utilization 50 and 60
fresh attention-named design paths to avoid reusing terminal-softmax metrics silently
exclude: cycle-accurate SRAM buffering around the softmax block

expected_benefit: turns the softmax-weight-generation abstraction into measured PPA inputs
brackets the cost difference between q8 integer and q12 PWL softmax paths
provides the required profile inputs for the next L2 full-value attention rerun
risks: row_elems=8 is a lane-level generator contract and must be scaled carefully into the tile-level L2 model
the PWL blocks may close with large critical paths and need later pipelining before integration
quality impact is not measured in this L1 PPA job

required_evaluations.objective: Measure attention-named q8 and q12 rowwise softmax weight-generator PPA variants for the Llama7B attention schedule.

expected_result.reason: Use the selected measured softmax profile to rerun the L2 full-value clustered attention schedule.
